reviews.title: love it
reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Review for the Bose SoundLink Max

The Bose SoundLink Max is honestly one of the best portable speakers I’ve owned. The sound quality is powerful, clear, and rich with deep bass without sounding distorted, even at high volume. Whether indoors or outside, it fills the space perfectly.

The battery life is excellent and lasts for hours, making it great for trips, BBQs, cabins, or relaxing outside. I also love the premium build quality — it feels durable, stylish, and easy to carry. The Bluetooth connection is fast and reliable, and the sound stays consistent even when moving around.

What impressed me most is how balanced the audio sounds. Vocals are crisp, bass hits hard, and music sounds immersive no matter the genre. Bose really did an amazing job combining portability with high-end sound.

Definitely worth the investment if you want a portable speaker that sounds like a much bigger system.

reviews.title: Really good speaker
reviews.text: I really like this speaker. I used it indoors mostly. My friends and I used it for dungeons and dragons sound and music and everyone agreed the quality was very good. It has a very clear quality to it and can be heard without feeling super loud, but you can also really get it loud. It can play super quietly, just below a whisper, as well without losing clarity. The battery life is also really good. It was at 50% battery and we used it for 3 4 hour d&d sessions and it still never died.

reviews.author: apple

reviews.title: Battery Discharge flaw
reviews.text: This speaker has a design flaw with parasitic battery discharge (possibly from Bluetooth staying active) even after turning off the speaker. Check out forums online, it is a known issue that drains from 10-20% per week if the speaker is left unplugged. It's forgivable for a $25 Temu junker, but not this. Anyway mine drained to 0% (not knowing this) and now it won't turn on anymore or charge the battery. Im getting it replaced *hopefully* though Bose warranty, but 3-4 week claims are an annoying chore to deal with for a 'premium' product. Missed the 15 day BB return window unfortunately. It sounded fantastic when it worked for the month I had it, but not worth the hassle. Buyer beware.
